content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 307 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 9581

Technical Assistance/Administration · · · Executive Legal · · · Enforcement · · · Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                322-5901            322-6441            322-6444

February 23, 1978


William H. Lewis, Jr.
Executive Officer
Air Resources Board
1102 Q Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Opinion Request
    No. 77-010

Dear Mr. Lewis:

     At its meeting on February 7, 1978, the Fair Political
Practices Commission voted to dismiss the Opinion Request
you submitted on behalf of Peter Venturini asking whether he
could avoid disqualification by placing his assets in a
blind trust. This action was taken because the Commission
decided to cover blind trusts in a proposed regulation on
reporting interests in trusts. That proposed regulation is
expected to be considered by the Commission at its meeting
on April 4-6, 1978.

     It was the unanimous consensus of the Commission
that your specific question could be answered as follows:
Mr. Venturini should continue to disqualify himself from
working on those projects which may have a material financial
effect on his interests in Sinco Oil Corporation ("Sinco")
as long as he owns Sinco stock. His proposed establishment
of a trust for those assets will not eliminate the need for
disqualification as long as the Sinco assets are owned.
Mr. Venturini can avoid the necessity for disqualification
only by selling the Sinco assets which constitute the financial
interest.

     While the standards for the use of a blind trust
under the Political Reform Act have not yet been determined,
it is certain that, at a minimum, an individual would have
to disclose assets which were transferred into a blind trust

William H. Lewis, Jr.
February 23, 1978
Page Two


and continue to disqualify himself or herself as long as
those assets were owned by the trust.

     Thank you for bringing a difficult issue to our
attention. I regret any inconvenience which the dismissal
may have caused you, your agency or Mr. Venturini. If you
have any questions, please contact Barbara Campbell, an
attorney in our Legal Division.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [illegible]
                                        Michael Bennett
                                        Executive Director

MB:BC:plh

sections.conclusion: Mr. Venturini should continue to disqualify himself from working on those projects which may have a material financial effect on his interests in Sinco Oil Corporation as long as he owns Sinco stock. His proposed establishment of a trust for those assets will not eliminate the need for disqualification as long as the Sinco assets are owned. Mr. Venturini can avoid the necessity for disqualification only by selling the Sinco assets which constitute the financial interest. While the standards for the use of a blind trust under the Political Reform Act have not yet been determined, it is certain that, at a minimum, an individual would have to disclose assets which were transferred into a blind trust and continue to disqualify himself or herself as long as those assets were owned by the trust.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Peter Venturini avoid disqualification from working on certain projects by placing his assets in a blind trust?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that placing assets in a blind trust does not eliminate the disqualification requirement; Mr. Venturini must continue to disqualify himself from projects affecting Sinco Oil assets as long as he owns them, or sell the assets entirely. The FPPC deferred formal standards for blind trust use to a proposed regulation expected in April 1978.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/dismissal letter with consensus advice provided. The document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains substantive question and answer content in the body letter. Minor OCR errors present (e.g., 'disquality' instead of 'disqualify'). Opinion Request number 77-010 referenced.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can Peter Venturini avoid disqualification from working on certain projects by placing his assets in a blind trust?

CONCLUSION: Mr. Venturini should continue to disqualify himself from working on those projects which may have a material financial effect on his interests in Sinco Oil Corporation as long as he owns Sinco stock. His proposed establishment of a trust for those assets will not eliminate the need for disqualification as long as the Sinco assets are owned. Mr. Venturini can avoid the necessity for disqualification only by selling the Sinco assets which constitute the financial interest. While the standards for the use of a blind trust under the Political Reform Act have not yet been determined, it is certain that, at a minimum, an individual would have to disclose assets which were transferred into a blind trust and continue to disqualify himself or herself as long as those assets were owned by the trust.

embedding.summary: The FPPC dismissed Opinion Request No. 77-010 from William H. Lewis regarding Peter Venturini's proposed blind trust arrangement, determining that asset placement in a blind trust would not eliminate disqualification requirements and that the opinion would be addressed in forthcoming regulations on trust reporting.
